categories.name: Financial Support
categories.description: Schemes related to money, subsidies, insurance, and financial assistance

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / NABARD
schemes.description: Provides short-term credit to farmers for crop cultivation, post-harvest expenses, produce marketing, and allied activities at subsidised interest rates.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must have cultivable land or be a tenant farmer or sharecropper

schemes.benefit: Revolving credit up to Rs 3 lakh at 4% interest (7% minus 3% govt subvention); livestock farmers up to Rs 2 lakh
schemes.how_to_apply: Visit nearest bank branch (SBI, PNB, cooperative bank) or apply online via bank's website

schemes.name: Agricultural Term Loan (ATL) – NABARD
schemes.description: Long-term loans for land development, irrigation, farm mechanisation, plantation crops, and allied activities.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must have a viable project plan

schemes.benefit: Loans from Rs 50,000 up to Rs 25 lakh; repayment tenure 3–15 years depending on activity
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), cooperative banks, or NABARD-linked institutions

schemes.name: PM Mudra Yojana – Kishor & Tarun (Agri)
schemes.description: Collateral-free loans for agri-allied activities like poultry, dairy, fishery, food processing micro-enterprises.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Non-farm agri-allied or food processing micro enterprise

schemes.benefit: Kishor: Rs 50,001–Rs 5 lakh; Tarun: Rs 5 lakh–Rs 10 lakh; no collateral required
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at any scheduled bank, MFI, NBFC or online at mudra.org.in

schemes.name: National Food Security Mission (NFSM) – Input Subsidy
schemes.description: Provides subsidised certified seeds, micronutrients, soil amendments, and plant protection chemicals to boost rice, wheat, pulses, and coarse cereals production.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Farmer must be in NFSM-notified district

schemes.benefit: Free certified seeds (HYV), 50% subsidy on micronutrients and soil conditioners, subsidised pesticides and farm demos
documents_required: Farmer registration at local agriculture office
schemes.how_to_apply: Register at the Block Agriculture Officer or Krishi Vigyan Kendra in your district

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance for purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment at subsidised rates.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST, small & marginal, women farmers get priority; FPO members also eligible

schemes.benefit: 40–50% subsidy for general farmers; up to 80% subsidy for SC/ST and women farmers on tractors, tillers, harvesters, and irrigation equipment
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture department portal or at district agriculture office

schemes.name: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi
schemes.description: Direct income support of Rs 6,000 per year to small and marginal farmer families having cultivable land, payable in three equal installments of Rs 2,000 every four months.

eligibility.exclusions: Income tax payers, constitutional post holders, central/state govt employees (except MTS/Class IV), institutional landholders
eligibility.additional_criteria: Land must be in farmer's or family's name as per land records

schemes.benefit: Rs 6,000 per year in 3 installments of Rs 2,000 each directly to bank account via DBT
schemes.how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or through Common Service Centre (CSC) or state agriculture department

schemes.name: Rythu Bandhu – Investment Support Scheme
schemes.description: Telangana state scheme providing pre-season agriculture investment support to all farmers before each crop season.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must be a land-owning farmer in Telangana; pattadar passbook required

schemes.benefit: Rs 10,000 per acre per year (Rs 5,000 per season) for Kharif and Rabi seasons
schemes.how_to_apply: Automatic disbursement to registered farmers; new registration at MeeSeva centres or agriculture office

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.description: Comprehensive crop insurance covering pre-sowing, standing crop, and post-harvest losses due to non-preventable natural risks.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must grow notified crops in notified areas; KCC holders enrolled automatically; voluntary for non-loanee farmers

schemes.benefit: Full sum insured for crop loss; farmer premium: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial/horticulture crops; rest paid by govt
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at nearest bank (if KCC holder, auto-enrolled) or at Common Service Centre or online at pmfby.gov.in

schemes.name: Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)
schemes.description: Insurance against adverse weather events (rainfall deficit, excess rain, frost, heat, humidity) that cause crop losses, using automatic weather station data.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Available for notified horticultural and food crops in notified districts

schemes.benefit: Automatic payout triggered by weather parameters without crop loss assessment; same premium structure as PMFBY
schemes.how_to_apply: Enroll at bank branch or CSC; KCC holders automatically enrolled for notified crops

schemes.name: Livestock Insurance Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying
schemes.description: Insurance cover to milch animals (cattle and buffalo) against death due to accident or disease, protecting farmers from financial loss.

eligibility.additional_criteria: BPL and SC/ST farmers get subsidised premium; animals must be healthy and identified with permanent ear tags

schemes.benefit: Insurance cover equal to market value of animal; 50% premium subsidy for BPL/SC/ST farmers; maximum 5 animals per beneficiary
documents_required: Animal purchase receipt or valuation certificate
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at the district Animal Husbandry Department office or nearest livestock insurance agency

schemes.name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
schemes.description: Medium to long-term debt financing for post-harvest management infrastructure and community farming assets through interest subvention and credit guarantee.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Project must be for post-harvest infrastructure: cold storage, warehousing, sorting, grading, primary processing

schemes.benefit: Loans up to Rs 2 crore with 3% interest subvention; credit guarantee from NCGTC for loans up to Rs 2 crore
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at agriinfra.dac.gov.in or through participating banks and cooperative institutions

categories.name: Farming Infrastructure
categories.description: Schemes that help improve farming facilities and productivity

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti / Ministry of Agriculture
schemes.description: Comprehensive irrigation scheme with the goal of 'Har Khet Ko Pani' (water to every field) and 'More Crop Per Drop' through efficient water use technologies.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Priority to districts with groundwater depletion; SC/ST and women farmers get additional 10% subsidy

schemes.benefit: 55% subsidy on drip/sprinkler irrigation for small & marginal farmers; 45% for other farmers; 100% for SC/ST in some states
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at district agriculture office or state horticulture department; check state portal for per-drip component subsidy

schemes.name: PM-KUSUM (Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.description: Provides solar pumps and grid-connected solar power plants to farmers for reliable and affordable irrigation and additional income from solar energy.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Component B: existing diesel/electric pump owners; Component A: barren/fallow land 0.5 acre minimum

schemes.benefit: Component A: 30% central + 30% state subsidy on solar power plant (2 MW); Component B: 60% subsidy on solar pump (up to 7.5 HP); Component C: subsidy to solarise existing grid pumps
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at state DISCOM office or state renewable energy agency; or at state agriculture department portal

schemes.name: AMRUT – Agri Cold Chain Infrastructure
schemes.description: Financial assistance for setting up integrated cold chain and value addition infrastructure from farm gate to consumer.
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs 10 crore (50% of project cost for general; 75% for NE/hilly states); for cold storage, pre-cooling, ripening chambers
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at mofpi.gov.in or through state agriculture/food processing department

schemes.name: National Watershed Management (PMKSY-WDC)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Rural Development / Ministry of Agriculture
schemes.description: Integrated watershed development for sustainable livelihoods including soil and moisture conservation, land development, and ground water recharge.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Applicable in notified watershed areas; community-based approach; NREGA convergence for works

schemes.benefit: Construction of check dams, farm ponds, contour bunding, trenches; free for BPL and SC/ST farmers; livelihood support of Rs 3,000–6,000 per household
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through Gram Panchayat or block-level Watershed Development Team (WDT)

schemes.name: Custom Hiring Centre (CHC) under SMAM
schemes.description: Establishes Custom Hiring Centres with a fleet of farm machinery so small farmers can hire equipment affordably without owning it.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Priority to co-operative societies and FPOs; viable business plan required for CHC setup

schemes.benefit: 40% subsidy for setting up CHC (equipment pool of Rs 25 lakh); 80% subsidy for SC/ST; banks provide 60% as loan; farm machinery hiring at subsidised rates for members
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at state agriculture department or through agrimachinery.nic.in portal

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.description: Provides soil health cards to farmers with information on soil nutrient status and recommendations on appropriate dosage of nutrients for improved productivity.
schemes.benefit: Free soil testing every 2 years; personalised nutrient recommendation card; reduces fertilizer cost by 8–10% with better yield
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact Block Agriculture Officer or Krishi Vigyan Kendra; or register at soilhealth.dac.gov.in

categories.name: Agriculture & Crop Development
categories.description: Schemes focused on cultivation, crop improvement, and sustainable farming

schemes.name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
schemes.description: Promotes organic farming in cluster mode; provides financial support for certification, capacity building, and marketing of organic produce.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Farmers must form clusters of minimum 50 acres (20 farmers); three-year transition period to full organic

schemes.benefit: Rs 50,000/ha over 3 years (Rs 31,000 for farm inputs + Rs 8,800 for PGS certification + Rs 10,200 for value addition & marketing)
schemes.how_to_apply: Form a cluster with neighbours; apply through state agriculture department or Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana district nodal officer

schemes.name: Bharatiya Prakritik Krishi Paddhati (BPKP)
schemes.description: Promotes traditional Indian natural farming practices (zero budget natural farming) using Jivamrit, Bijamrit, and mulching; no external inputs.
schemes.benefit: Rs 12,200/ha for cluster demonstrations; free training, exposure visits, capacity building; cost savings from zero external inputs
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or state agriculture department for registration

schemes.name: NFSM Seed Village Programme
schemes.description: Provides quality certified seeds of HYV and hybrid varieties at subsidised rates to create a network of seed villages for local seed production.
schemes.benefit: Seeds at 50% subsidised cost for paddy, wheat, pulses; free demonstration plots; 10–15% yield increase expected
schemes.how_to_apply: Register at district agriculture office or block agriculture officer

schemes.name: Neem Coated Urea Policy
schemes.description: 100% urea must be neem coated; reduces misuse, improves nitrogen use efficiency, and reduces cost of cultivation.
schemes.benefit: Neem coated urea at same MRP as regular urea; 5–10% better yield; available at all fertilizer retail shops
documents_required: Kisan credit card or farmer ID for subsidised purchase
schemes.how_to_apply: Available at all licensed fertilizer dealers; no separate application needed

schemes.name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)
schemes.description: Holistic development of horticulture sector covering fruits, vegetables, root & tuber crops, mushrooms, spices, flowers, aromatic plants, coconut, cashew, and bamboo.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST, small & marginal, NE states get higher subsidy; protected cultivation subsidy for poly houses

schemes.benefit: 40–50% subsidy on establishment of orchards, poly houses, shade nets; 50% for bee colonies; 33–50% for pack houses and sorting units
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at state horticulture department or National Horticulture Board district office

schemes.name: Minimum Support Price (MSP) Procurement – FCI / State Agencies
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture / Food Corporation of India
schemes.description: Government procures 23 major crops at announced MSP to protect farmers from distress selling; wheat and paddy procured at scale through FCI and state agencies.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Farmer must be registered; produce must meet FAQ (Fair Average Quality) specifications; sale through mandis or direct procurement centres

schemes.benefit: Assured price for 23 crops including paddy, wheat, pulses, oilseeds, cotton; payment directly to bank account within 3 days of delivery
schemes.how_to_apply: Register at nearest APMC / mandi or state procurement portal; take produce to designated procurement centre during notified dates

schemes.name: PM-AASHA (Annadata Aay SanraksHan Abhiyan)
schemes.description: Umbrella scheme ensuring remunerative prices to oilseed, pulses, and copra farmers through MSP-based procurement and price support.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Only for notified crops: oilseeds, pulses, copra; price difference paid when market price falls below MSP

schemes.benefit: Price Deficiency Payment (PDP) of difference between MSP and market price directly to bank account; no physical procurement burden on farmer
schemes.how_to_apply: Register on state agriculture or mandi portal; sell in APMC; deficiency payment auto-credited

schemes.name: Digital Agriculture Mission (DAM)
schemes.description: Creates digital public infrastructure for agriculture including AgriStack (farmer registry, crop sown registry, geo-referenced maps) for targeted service delivery.
schemes.benefit: Free digital Farmer ID (Kisan ID); access to personalised advisories, scheme eligibility alerts via SMS; precision farming support through satellite data
schemes.how_to_apply: Enrol for Kisan ID at Common Service Centre or state agriculture department; Kisan e-mitra chatbot assistance available

categories.name: Allied Farming Activities
categories.description: Schemes related to animal farming, fisheries, and related sectors

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission (NLM)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying
schemes.description: Sustainable development of livestock sector with focus on breed improvement, fodder development, and risk management for small farmers and rural entrepreneurs.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST and women get priority; individual or SHG/cooperative group

schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy for individual farmers; 33% for companies; subsidy on breed improvement, fodder machines, silage making, livestock shelter
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at district Animal Husbandry office or state Animal Husbandry Department

schemes.name: Dairy Entrepreneurship Development Scheme (DEDS)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying / NABARD
schemes.description: Capital investment subsidy scheme for setting up modern dairy farms and ancillary units for milk processing and preservation.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST get 33.33% subsidy; others get 25%; minimum 2 animals up to 10 for individual

schemes.benefit: 25% capital subsidy (33.33% for SC/ST) on project cost for 2–10 animal dairy unit, milk cooling, chilling equipment; max subsidy Rs 1.67 lakh to Rs 20 lakh
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at NABARD district office or through scheduled commercial bank for loan + subsidy

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying
schemes.description: Holistic development of fisheries sector covering capture fisheries, aquaculture, post-harvest management, and cold chain development.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST and women get higher subsidy (extra 10%); freshwater, brackishwater, marine all eligible

schemes.benefit: 40% subsidy for general (60% for SC/ST/Women) on fish ponds, cages, boat/net, cold chain, feed; Rs 20,050 crore total outlay till 2025
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at district fisheries office or state fisheries development board; online at pmmsy.dof.gov.in

schemes.name: National Backyard Poultry Development Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying
schemes.description: Provides improved breeds of backyard poultry (Vanaraja, Gramapriya) to rural households, particularly tribal and BPL families for nutritional and income security.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Priority to tribal, BPL, landless and women-headed households; 40 chicks per unit

schemes.benefit: Free chicks (40 per household) of improved dual-purpose breeds; free feed for 4 weeks; free vaccination; training on backyard poultry management
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact district Animal Husbandry office or veterinary hospital

schemes.name: National Beekeeping & Honey Mission (NBHM)
schemes.description: Comprehensive development of beekeeping for pollination services, honey production, income diversification, and 'Sweet Revolution'.

eligibility.additional_criteria: SC/ST, tribal, and women beekeepers get priority and higher subsidy; training completion required

schemes.benefit: Subsidy on bee boxes (Rs 2,000 per box), bee colonies, honey extractors, bee veil, smoker; 40% subsidy general, 60% for SC/ST/women; free 5-day training
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture department or National Bee Board state office

categories.name: Training & Market Support
categories.description: Schemes that help farmers learn, upskill, and access better markets

schemes.name: ATMA – Agricultural Technology Management Agency
schemes.description: Decentralised extension system at district level providing training, demonstrations, exposure visits, Kisan Melas, and field days to disseminate improved agricultural technology.
schemes.benefit: Free training (1–7 day courses), field demonstrations, farm visits, Kisan Melas; exposure visits to agricultural institutions; Rs 1,000–2,000 per day stipend for residential training
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact Block Technology Team (BTT) at block agriculture office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) in your district

schemes.name: Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) – Skill Development
schemes.description: Farm science centres at district level for vocational training, on-farm testing of technologies, and front-line demonstrations for farmers and rural youth.
schemes.benefit: Free vocational training (1–3 months) in agri-allied trades; on-farm technology testing; free advisory; dial a KVK helpline; ICAR-certified training
documents_required: Educational certificate (class 8 min. for vocational training)
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact nearest KVK (one per district across India) directly; kvk.icar.gov.in for district-wise list

schemes.name: eNAM – National Agriculture Market
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / SFAC
schemes.description: Online trading platform linking 1,000+ mandis across India for transparent price discovery, reduced intermediaries, and better farmer realisation.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Produce must be sold at eNAM-integrated APMCs; farmer must have bank account

schemes.benefit: Better price discovery through online bidding; payment within 24 hours to bank account; quality assaying at mandi; access to buyers across states
schemes.how_to_apply: Register at enam.gov.in or at APMC mandi office in your district

schemes.name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
schemes.description: Credit-linked capital subsidy and support for existing micro food processing enterprises to enhance their competitiveness and integration into the formal economy.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Existing food processing unit or new unit by farmer; ODOP (One District One Product) alignment preferred

schemes.benefit: 35% credit-linked subsidy (max Rs 10 lakh per unit) for upgradation; SHGs: Rs 40,000 working capital grant + Rs 4 lakh equipment subsidy; seed capital Rs 40,000 for new units
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at pmfme.mofpi.gov.in or through state nodal agency

schemes.name: Formation & Promotion of 10,000 FPOs
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / SFAC / NABARD / NCDC
schemes.description: Financial support for formation, promotion, and handholding of 10,000 Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs) to improve farmers' bargaining power and market access.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Minimum 300 members for plains; 100 for NE/hilly states; must register as FPC (Farmer Producer Company) under Companies Act

schemes.benefit: Rs 18 lakh grant per FPO over 5 years for management cost; equity grant up to Rs 15 lakh; credit guarantee up to Rs 2 crore; training and handholding support
schemes.how_to_apply: Form FPO with minimum members; approach SFAC, NABARD, or NCDC as Cluster Based Business Organisation (CBBO)

schemes.name: RKVY-RAFTAAR – Agri Startup Programme
schemes.description: Incubation and funding support for agri-startups and rural entrepreneurs to develop innovative solutions for the agriculture sector.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must have an agri-tech innovation idea; apply to any of 25 RKVY-RAFTAAR Agri Business Incubators (R-ABIs)

schemes.benefit: Seed funding up to Rs 5 lakh (pre-seed) and Rs 25 lakh (seed stage); free incubation for 2 years; mentoring, training, market access, networking support
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply to any of 25 RKVY-RAFTAAR Agri Business Incubators (R-ABIs) online; rkvy.nic.in/static/raftaar.html

categories.name: Special Farmer Categories
categories.description: Schemes targeted to specific farmer groups with enhanced support

schemes.name: Mahila Kisan Sashaktikaran Pariyojana (MKSP)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Rural Development – NRLM / DAY-NRLM
schemes.description: Empowers women farmers by improving their participation and productivity in agriculture; promotes SHG-based interventions for inputs, credit, technology, and market access.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must be member of a SHG (Self Help Group) linked to NRLM; preference for SC/ST/minority women

schemes.benefit: SHG revolving fund Rs 10,000–15,000; Community Investment Fund up to Rs 2.5 lakh per SHG; training in organic farming, nutrition garden, seed bank; market linkage support
schemes.how_to_apply: Join NRLM SHG through Gram Panchayat or Block NRLM office; register at aajeevika.gov.in

schemes.name: SMAM – Enhanced Subsidy for Women Farmers
schemes.description: Under Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanisation, women farmers receive higher subsidy (up to 80%) on farm equipment, tools, and machinery.
schemes.benefit: Up to 80% subsidy on tractors, power tillers, reapers, threshers, sprayers for women farmers; higher priority in selection
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at district agriculture office with land records showing women's ownership or co-ownership

schemes.name: RKVY-RAFTAAR – Young Farmer Agri-Entrepreneur
schemes.description: Special focus within RKVY-RAFTAAR to attract educated rural youth and young farmers into agriculture through skill training and startup funding.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Educated youth (Class 10+) from agricultural families; agriculture or agri-allied innovation idea preferred

schemes.benefit: Seed funding Rs 5–25 lakh; 2 years free incubation; Rs 1 lakh training stipend; mentoring from successful agri-entrepreneurs
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at rkvy.nic.in to nearest R-ABI incubator

schemes.name: PMKVY – Skill Development for Young Farmers
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana provides short-term skill training in agriculture and allied sectors for rural youth with certification and placement support.

eligibility.additional_criteria: School dropout, unemployed youth, and farmers' children; no minimum education for most agri courses

schemes.benefit: Free short-term training (150–300 hrs) in organic farming, dairy, poultry, fisheries, food processing; Skill India certificate; placement linkage; Rs 500–1,500 post-training incentive
schemes.how_to_apply: Find nearest PMKVY training centre at skillindia.gov.in or call skill helpline

schemes.name: SFAC – Venture Capital Assistance for Small Farmers
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / SFAC
schemes.description: Provides venture capital assistance to small and marginal farmers as interest-free loan for agri-business projects through FPOs and cooperatives.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Project must be through an FPO/cooperative; individual farmers can apply through their FPO

schemes.benefit: Interest-free venture capital Rs 25 lakh per project; for post-harvest, processing, cold chain; repayable over 5–7 years
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through FPO to SFAC regional office or at sfacindia.com

schemes.name: PMKSY-PDMC – Micro Irrigation for Small Farmers
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / NABARD
schemes.description: Special micro irrigation fund of Rs 5,000 crore at NABARD to extend benefits of drip and sprinkler irrigation to small and marginal farmers beyond normal PMKSY allocations.
schemes.benefit: Soft loans to states at 3% interest to extend higher subsidy (70–80%) to small farmers on drip and sprinkler systems; bridging the subsidy gap over PMKSY norms
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture or horticulture department micro-irrigation portal

schemes.name: SC Sub-Plan (SCSP) & Tribal Sub-Plan (TSP) – Agriculture
schemes.description: Earmarked funds within all centrally sponsored agriculture schemes for SC and ST farmers to ensure proportionate benefit flow with enhanced subsidy rates.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Valid SC/ST certificate; priority in all agriculture scheme beneficiary lists

schemes.benefit: Additional 10–20% subsidy on all major agriculture schemes; priority allotment of subsidised inputs, KCC, PMFBY, SMAM equipment; dedicated extension services
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply to any agriculture scheme mentioning SC/ST category; automatic priority given; approach district agriculture office for dedicated camp

schemes.name: WADI Programme – NABARD (Tribal Horticulture)
schemes.description: Develops small horticulture and forestry-based livelihoods for tribal farming families through plantation of fruit trees (Wadi = small orchard) with convergence of multiple schemes.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Exclusively for ST (Scheduled Tribe) farmers in identified tribal belts; must have 0.5–3 acres of land

schemes.benefit: Free fruit tree saplings (mango, cashew, amla); Rs 15,000–20,000 per acre for orchard establishment; 5 years of handholding, training, and market linkage
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact NABARD district office or tribal welfare department; implemented through NGO-NABARD partnership

schemes.name: TRIFED – Minor Forest Produce MSP & Tribal Farmers
schemes.description: Ensures fair prices for Minor Forest Produce (MFP) collectors and tribal farmers through MSP mechanism, value addition, and market linkage under Van Dhan Vikas Kendras.

eligibility.additional_criteria: Must be tribal forest dwellers or ST farmers; applicable for MFP collection and sale

schemes.benefit: MSP for 87 minor forest produce items (Mahua, Tamarind, Sal leaves, Bamboo, etc.); processing support through Van Dhan Vikas Kendras; 15–20% value addition profit
documents_required: Forest Rights Act certificate if available
schemes.how_to_apply: Register with Van Dhan Vikas Kendra (VDVK) in your tribal area; contact state TRIFED office or tribal welfare department

eligibility_quick_reference.small_marginal_farmer: Land size <= 2 acres qualifies as marginal; 2–5 acres = small farmer
eligibility_quick_reference.sc_st_benefit: Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe farmers get 10–20% additional subsidy across all schemes and priority selection
eligibility_quick_reference.women_farmer_benefit: Women farmers get priority selection, higher subsidy (up to 80% under SMAM), and dedicated schemes like MKSP
eligibility_quick_reference.young_farmer: Age < 35 qualifies for RKVY-RAFTAAR startup support and PMKVY skill training
eligibility_quick_reference.kcc_auto_enrollment: KCC holders are automatically enrolled for PMFBY crop insurance for notified crops
